packageRules.description: Ignore pnpm workspace: protocol deps. Renovate skips them natively — rule kept for explicit intent.
peerDependencies are a compatibility contract with consumers, not an installed dep. Bumping them is a deliberate API decision tied to a plugin major.
Extra cooldown for majors
cargo: group minor/patch; majors stay individual
cargo: ungroup pre-1.0 minors — Cargo treats 0.x→0.y as breaking, so these need their own PR.
npm: group minor/patch; majors stay individual
npm: ungroup pre-1.0 minors — ^0.x.y resolves like ~0.x.y, so 0.x→0.y is breaking and needs its own PR.
github-actions: group everything, including majors

Group all alloy crates together (including the op-alloy Optimism variants used by edr_op) — alloy's API often shifts across versions, op-alloy carries alloy-* deps transitively, so the bundle should land as one PR for review.
Group revm and the Optimism revm variant together — tightly coupled API surface, perf-sensitive.
rayon: parallelism crate, perf-sensitive. Land isolated.
reqwest and its middleware/retry siblings — HTTP client used in the RPC fork path; perf-sensitive on remote-block flows. Land grouped.
napi-rs: ABI-tied across edr_napi's Rust crates and JS toolchain. napi/napi-derive/napi-build (cargo) and @napi-rs/cli (npm) must move in lockstep.
tokio runtime family — tokio + tokio-stream/tokio-util/tokio-tungstenite. Land grouped to avoid version skew across async surfaces.
tracing ecosystem — tracing + tracing-subscriber/tracing-futures/tracing-error. Land grouped to keep span/event API consistent.

External foundry-rs library crates (not vendored — fetched from crates.io). foundry-compilers/foundry-block-explorers/foundry-fork-db often release together upstream and may need lockstep with the vendored Foundry fork in crates/foundry/.
Block Node major bumps across every surface (.nvmrc, engines.node, @types/node, actions/setup-node's node-version). Bumped manually, only to even LTS releases.
@tsconfig/nodeXX: the Node major lives in the package name (node20 → node22 = Node 20 → 22). Renovate models these as 'replacement' updates — block them alongside the Node-major lockdown above.
Block typescript majors. TS majors cascade to typescript-eslint and to consumers of EDR's TS-aware bindings — bump manually when the ecosystem catches up.

Pinned cargo git-source deps: slang_solidity is tag-pinned, revm-inspectors is a Wodann fork at a specific rev. Disable Renovate's default git-digest updates — these are bumped manually for compat. Note: matches on depName (Cargo.toml key), not packageName (which is the git URL for git-source deps).

Hardhat ecosystem npm deps (hardhat, hardhat2 alias, @nomicfoundation/hardhat-*) are exact-pinned, patched (pnpm patchedDependencies key includes the version), and partly on pre-release tracks. Bumps invalidate patches and break the v2/v3 side-by-side benchmark setup. Bumped manually as part of coordinated edr↔hardhat releases.
